name: torch.compile
description: Top-level compilation API — entry point for PT2

name: TorchDynamo
description: Python bytecode analysis and graph capture engine

name: TorchInductor
description: Default compiler backend — generates Triton/C++ kernels

name: AOTAutograd
description: Ahead-of-time autograd tracing for backward pass capture

name: AOT Inductor
description: Ahead-of-time compilation for deployment — generates standalone artifacts

name: FX Graph / FX IR
description: Intermediate representation used between dynamo and backends

name: Triton
description: GPU kernel language used by inductor backend

name: torch.export
description: Model export for deployment — produces ExportedProgram

name: Guard System
description: Validates assumptions about tensor shapes, dtypes, values for cache reuse

name: Dynamic Shapes
description: System for handling variable-length dimensions without recompilation

name: PT2 CUDAGraph
description: CUDA graph capture and replay integration with torch.compile

name: Compilation Cache
description: Caching system for compiled artifacts (FXGraphCache, Triton cache, remote cache)

name: Custom Ops
description: User-defined operators registered with torch.library for compile compatibility

name: Higher Order Ops
description: Ops that take functions as arguments (e.g., torch.cond, torch.vmap, checkpoint)

name: FlexAttention
description: Flexible attention API compiled through inductor for custom attention patterns

name: Inductor CuteDSL Backend
description: CuteDSL-based kernel generation backend for inductor

name: Max-Autotune
description: Autotuning system that benchmarks multiple kernel implementations to select the fastest

name: Compiled Optimizer
description: torch.compile applied to optimizer step for fused parameter updates

name: PT2 Debuggability & Observability
description: Logging, minifier, debug tools, compile profiling, graph visualization

name: Compiled Tensor Subclass
description: Support for tensor subclasses (DTensor, NF4Tensor, etc.) through compilation

name: Custom FX Passes
description: User-defined or built-in FX graph transformation passes

name: Pattern Matcher
description: Inductor's pattern matching system for graph-level optimizations (fusion, lowering)

name: Fake Tensor
description: Meta-device tensor proxy used during tracing to track shapes without data

name: Functionalization
description: Pass that converts in-place and view ops to functional equivalents for tracing

name: Decompositions
description: Operator decomposition layer — breaks complex ops into simpler primitives for backend consumption

name: Inductor Scheduler
description: Schedules and fuses kernels in the inductor backend — decides what to fuse, reorder, and overlap

name: Triton Heuristics
description: Heuristic system for selecting Triton kernel configs (tile sizes, num_warps, RBLOCK, XBLOCK)

name: Coordinate Descent Tuning
description: Advanced autotuning that searches the config space via coordinate descent for optimal kernel performance

name: Selective Activation Checkpointing
description: Fine-grained activation checkpointing integrated with torch.compile to reduce memory

name: SimpleFSDP
description: Compiler-friendly version of FSDP designed for full graph capture and compiler toolkit workflows

name: Compiler Toolkit
description: Toolkit-style usage of compiler stack — AOT full joint-graph capture with user-controlled passes, regional inductor, bucketing

name: Graph Partitioner
description: Partitions captured graphs into subgraphs for regional compilation, CUDAGraph splitting, etc.

name: Standalone Compile
description: torch._inductor.standalone_compile — simplified compile entry point for specific use cases like vLLM

name: tlparse
description: Log parsing and visualization tool for torch.compile debug logs

name: Compile Time Profiler
description: Profiling infrastructure for compile time breakdown — identifying where compile time is spent

name: Compute/Comm Overlap
description: Inductor passes for reordering compute and communication ops to overlap them in distributed training

name: Combo Kernels
description: Inductor technique for combining multiple small kernels into a single kernel to reduce launch overhead

name: Fusion Optimization
description: Inductor fusion strategies — horizontal fusion, vertical fusion, and fusion policies for combining operations

name: Inductor On Demand
description: Applying inductor compilation to specific annotated regions rather than the full graph — formerly 'regional compilation'
